steps.title: Identify what would weaken the hypothesis
steps.content: A hypothesis that acidic soil is better for growth is weakened by evidence that neutral soil actually produces superior growth.

steps.title: Find the choice showing neutral soil's growth superiority
steps.content: Choice A shows that plants in neutral soil grew significantly taller than those in acidic soil—a direct measure of growth advantage. Choices B, C, and D point the other direction (greater weight and more sprouting in acidic soil) or address germination timing rather than growth quality. That matches choice A — A is correct.
